title: ammophila littoralis
intro: Ammophila littoralis, also known as sea sandwort, is a type of succulent plant that grows in coastal areas and is known for its adaptability to sandy and saline environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ammophila littoralis include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its high content of vitamins and minerals. Herbal preparations of Ammophila littoralis may include infusions, decoctions, and creams, which can be used topically to soothe and heal various conditions.
